findings:
## DevSteps Write-Op Delegation Audit

### FILE 1: devsteps-R0-coord.agent.md
**Tools YAML:** `devsteps/*` wildcard (all write ops included). `worker-devsteps` in agents list (line 55).
**Line 173 (VIOLATION):** Operational Rules instructs coord to call `devsteps/add`, `devsteps/update`, `devsteps/link` directly for mid-item lifecycle and new-item discovery. No delegation to worker-devsteps for Planning/Backlog work types. Gap: no rule distinguishing lifecycle status ops (coord-OK) from batch item creation (should delegate).

### FILE 2: devsteps-R0-coord-sprint.agent.md
**Tools YAML:** `devsteps/*` wildcard. `worker-devsteps` in agents list (line 55).
**Line 184 (VIOLATION):** DevSteps Integration section instructs sprint-coord to call `devsteps/add`, `devsteps/update`, `devsteps/link` directly for lifecycle and new-item creation.
**Line 188 (VIOLATION):** `"New issue found → devsteps/search then devsteps/add before continuing"` — coord-direct add, no delegation rule.

### FILE 3: devsteps-R0-coord-solo.agent.md
**Line 64/72/73/74 (OK — by design):** `mcp_devsteps_add`, `mcp_devsteps_update`, `mcp_devsteps_link` all coord-direct. Solo mode has no runSubagent, worker-devsteps cannot be dispatched. Absent from agents list. Direct write ops are correct fallback behavior.

### FILE 4: devsteps-R4-worker-devsteps.agent.md
**(OK):** Correctly defined as exclusive delegatee. Tool-mapping table maps `add → mcp_devsteps_add`, `update → mcp_devsteps_update`, `link → mcp_devsteps_link`. `user-invokable: false`. No violations.

### FILE 5: AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md
**(GAP):** Invariants I-1 through I-10 exist but NO invariant prohibits coord from calling batch write ops directly for Planning/Backlog. Work Items spoke mapped to worker-devsteps in the radar table but no enforcement rule present.

### FILE 6: copilot-instructions.md
**(CONTRADICTION):** Lifecycle section (lines 55-56, 60) prescribes coord-direct `mcp_devsteps_add` and `mcp_devsteps_update`. Work-Type Dispatch Matrix (line 138, 141) prescribes delegation to worker-devsteps for Planning and Backlog hygiene work types. These directly contradict each other with no reconciliation rule.

### FILE 7: devsteps-agent-protocol.instructions.md
**(GAP):** Tool Reference table (lines 147-152) lists `mcp_devsteps_add`, `mcp_devsteps_update`, `mcp_devsteps_link` in coord-context instructions file without a 'Caller' column or delegation boundary rule.
recommendations: FIX copilot-instructions.md:52-61 — Clarify: lifecycle status ops (in-progress/done) are coord-direct; batch item creation for Planning/Backlog work types delegates to worker-devsteps.
FIX devsteps-R0-coord.agent.md:173 — Add rule: Planning/Backlog batch ops → dispatch worker-devsteps; lifecycle status transitions remain coord-direct on main.
FIX devsteps-R0-coord-sprint.agent.md:184,188 — Clarify: mid-sprint new item discovery for batch Planning uses worker-devsteps; lifecycle ops remain coord-direct.
ADD to AGENT-DISPATCH-PROTOCOL.md Invariants — New I-11: coord MUST NOT call mcp_devsteps_add/link for batch Planning/Backlog ops — delegate to worker-devsteps.
ADD to devsteps-agent-protocol.instructions.md Tool Reference — 'Caller' column: batch add/link → worker-devsteps; status updates → coord-direct.
